title: Fix interface and struct pattern collision (#5640)
body:
#5596 causes internal errors when there is a collision between struct field names and other identifiers (in this case an interface).  `t_interface_and_struct_pattern.py` demonstrates the problem:
```
%Error: Internal Error: t/t_interface_and_struct_pattern.v:38:23: ../V3Width.cpp:2318: Unlinked varref
                                                                : ... note: In instance 't.the_sub'
   38 |         bar: the_intf.data.foo,
      |                       ^~~~
```

`A.6.7.1` says:
```
array_pattern_key ::= constant_expression | assignment_pattern_key
```

So I believe my change is needed to enforce the constness.

@gregdavill I think this is necessary, but you may want to test this against your use case to make sure I haven't broken you.  However, the test from #5596 is still working.

@wsnyder I first tried to solve this with `AstVar::isConst()` but then I noticed this:
```
(rr) p AstNode::dumpGdb(varp)
This=VAR 0x555556eedd40 back=0x555556eede60 next=0x555557004000 headtail=0x55555700f290 op1p=0x555556f7b980 op3p=0x555556f7ca50 user1p=0x555556f80cf0
  Fileline = t/t_param_pattern_init.v:12:28: 
  VAR 0x555556eedd40 <e609> {f12bc} u1=0x555556f80cf0 @dt=0@  SPI_INDEX [VSTATIC]  LPARAM
$4 = void
(rr) p varp->m_isConst 
$5 = false
(rr) p varp->m_varType 
$6 = {m_e = VVarType::LPARAM}
```
which seems counterintuitive to me.  Why is a localparam not `isConst()`?

Also, you mentioned in #5631 about creating an issue and a PR.  I'm not quite sure if I understand the implementation details here.  Can you elaborate on what the right workflow is?  AKA, where should the main conversation be?  I the issue or in the PR?

Regardless, I'll open an issue in a minute which points at this guy.
